summary: Mitochondrial inheritance exclusively follows maternally through egg cells during fertilization; it does not involve paternal contribution or skip-generation occurrences typical of other genetic modes like autosomes. This unique pathway clearly distinguishes itself based on observed family histories where affected mothers pass conditions directly down their line without male intermediaries. The consistency across multiple studies confirms its distinctiveness compared to alternative forms such as X-linked traits that also exhibit sex-specific prevalence but do so differently due to crossover mechanisms allowing both sexes potential carrier status under certain circumstances absent here entirely within mtDNA lines traced back consistently along females' ancestry chains.
